title: Lost at the Airport
summary: A traveller asks an airport officer for help after leaving her bag on the bus.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Excuse me. I cannot find my bag.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: I am sorry to hear that. What does your bag look like? Can you describe it to me?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: It is small and red. It has a white star on the front.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: When did you last see it? Where were you?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: I put it on the bus. Then I came here to the airport.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: I see. What is inside the bag? Do you have any important things in it?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Yes. My phone, my keys, and my passport are in the bag.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Okay. Please write your name and phone number on this paper for me.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: My name is Maya. But my phone is in the bag! I do not have the number in my head.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: That is okay. Do you have a friend here with you?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Yes. My friend is outside. She has a phone. She can give you the number.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Good. We will call your friend when we find the bag. Please wait in the waiting area.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Thank you. Is this a common problem?

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Yes, many people forget their bags on the bus. We usually find them by the end of the day.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: That is good news. Thank you very much for your help.

annotations.phrase: by the end of the day

grammarNotes.title: Modal verb 'can'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'can' để nói về khả năng: 'Can you describe it?' / 'She can give you the number.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'can' to talk about ability: 'Can you describe it?' / 'She can give you the number.'

translationVi:
Maya: Xin lỗi. Tôi không thể tìm thấy túi của mình.
Nhân viên: Tôi rất tiếc khi nghe điều đó. Túi của bạn trông như thế nào? Bạn có thể mô tả nó cho tôi không?
Maya: Nó nhỏ và màu đỏ. Phía trước có một ngôi sao trắng.
Nhân viên: Lần cuối bạn nhìn thấy nó là khi nào? Bạn đang ở đâu?
Maya: Tôi để nó trên xe buýt. Sau đó tôi đến đây ở sân bay.
Nhân viên: Tôi hiểu. Bên trong túi có gì? Bạn có đồ quan trọng không?
Maya: Vâng. Điện thoại, chìa khóa và hộ chiếu của tôi đều trong túi.
Nhân viên: Được rồi. Vui lòng viết tên và số điện thoại của bạn lên tờ giấy này cho tôi.
Maya: Tên tôi là Maya. Nhưng điện thoại của tôi ở trong túi! Tôi không nhớ số trong đầu.
Nhân viên: Không sao. Bạn có người bạn nào ở đây không?
Maya: Có. Bạn tôi đang ở ngoài kia. Cô ấy có điện thoại. Cô ấy có thể đưa cho bạn số.
Nhân viên: Tốt. Chúng tôi sẽ gọi cho bạn của bạn khi tìm thấy túi. Vui lòng chờ ở khu vực chờ đợi.
Maya: Cảm ơn. Đây có phải là vấn đề phổ biến không?
Nhân viên: Vâng, nhiều người quên túi trên xe buýt. Chúng tôi thường tìm thấy trước cuối ngày.
Maya: Đó là tin tốt. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều vì đã giúp đỡ.

questions.prompt: Why does Maya stop the officer?
options: She wants information about the airport
She is looking for her friend

questions.hint: Find the sentence where Maya describes her bag.

questions.prompt: What is on the front of Maya's bag?
questions.explanation: 'It has a white star on the front.'

questions.prompt: Where did Maya leave her bag?
questions.hint: Find Maya's answer about where she last saw it.

options: Her jacket, her book, and her keys
Her phone, her keys, and her passport
Her money, her pen, and her glasses
Her food, her keys, and her ticket
questions.explanation: 'My phone, my keys, and my passport are in the bag.'
questions.hint: Listen for the list Maya gives.

questions.prompt: Why can Maya not write her phone number?
options: She does not have a pen
Her phone is in the bag
She does not speak English well
questions.explanation: 'But my phone is in the bag! I do not have the number in my head.'
'My friend is outside. She has a phone.'
questions.hint: Who can give the phone number?

questions.prompt: What should Maya do while she waits?
questions.hint: What does the officer tell Maya to do?

questions.prompt: Is it common to lose bags on the bus?
questions.explanation: 'Yes, many people forget their bags on the bus.'
questions.hint: What does the officer say about other people?

questions.prompt: When do they usually find lost bags?
options: By the end of the day
questions.explanation: 'We usually find them by the end of the day.'
questions.hint: Look at the officer's last long sentence.

questions.prompt: How does Maya feel at the end of the conversation?
questions.explanation: 'That is good news. Thank you very much for your help.'
questions.hint: What does Maya say in her last turn?

Maya: It is {{b3}} and red. It has a white star on the front.
Officer: When did you last see it? Where were you?

Officer: Okay. Please write your name and phone {{b7}} on this paper for me.
Maya: My name is Maya. But my phone is in the bag! I do not have the number in my {{b8}}.
Officer: That is okay. Do you have a {{b9}} here with you?
Maya: Yes. My friend is outside. She has a phone. She can give you the number.

Maya: Thank you. Is this a {{b11}} problem?
Officer: Yes, many people {{b12}} their bags on the bus. We usually find them by the end of the day.
Maya: That is good news. Thank you very much for your help.

blanks.explanation: 'My phone, my keys, and my passport are in the bag.'
'Please write your name and phone number on this paper.'
'I do not have the number in my head.'
'Do you have a friend here with you?'
'Many people forget their bags on the bus.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Maya's passport was in a bag she left on the bus. Why is it important to keep travel documents close to you? What habits help people stay safe when travelling?
